  Enumerate all sub-CIDRs of a given size within a CIDR
---

# function: cidr_enumerate

Returns every possible subnet of size `(parent prefix length + newbits)` within `cidr`. For example, `cidr_enumerate("10.0.0.0/24", 2)` returns all four /26 subnets within the /24. Returns an error if the result would exceed 65536 subnets.

**Common uses:** pre-computing all AZ-sized subnets within a region block for later `element()` selection; generating candidate subnets to feed into an IP address management workflow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Every subnet of a target size within a parent.
output "az_subnets" {
  value = provider::burnham::cidr_enumerate("10.0.0.0/24", 2)
  # → ["10.0.0.0/26", "10.0.0.64/26", "10.0.0.128/26", "10.0.0.192/26"]
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
cidr_enumerate(cidr string, newbits number) list of string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `cidr` (String) The parent CIDR to subdivide.
1. `newbits` (Number) Number of additional prefix bits. Each +1 halves the subnet size.
